commit.message:
fix: preserve key order in YAML output (#2034)

* fix: preserve key order in YAML output

MlrmapToYAMLNative converted the ordered Mlrmap to map[string]interface{},
which loses insertion order. yaml.v3.Marshal then sorts Go map keys
alphabetically, so YAML output always had sorted keys despite other formats
(JSON, pretty-print) correctly preserving order.

Switch to building yaml.Node trees that preserve the Mlrmap's linked-list
iteration order. Use yaml.Node.Encode for scalar values to correctly handle
edge cases (NaN/Inf, non-UTF-8 strings).

Fixes #2028.

* Update pkg/mlrval/mlrval_yaml_test.go with copilot code-review feedback

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Switch to musl-cross toolchain for Linux releases

The musl-native toolchain (x86_64-linux-musl-native) is itself linked
against musl libc and can't run on glibc systems (Ubuntu, Debian, etc).
Switch to x86_64-linux-musl-cross — same compiler output, but the
binary itself runs on any Linux.

Changes:
- linux-x86_64.json: URL points to Oscan's own GitHub releases (mirrored
  from musl.cc since musl.cc blocks GitHub Actions)
- release_tools.py: digest validation is now optional (null digest skips)
- mirror-toolchain.yml: manual workflow to download from musl.cc and
  upload to Oscan's toolchains release

Setup: run 'Mirror musl toolchain' workflow once, or manually:
  curl -fSL -o cross.tgz https://musl.cc/x86_64-linux-musl-cross.tgz
  gh release create toolchains --title Toolchains cross.tgz

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
fix: remove CSP meta tag that blocks Office.js iframe communication

The Content-Security-Policy meta tag added during security hardening was
blocking Office.js host-to-iframe communication, causing the add-in to
fail silently and not appear in Outlook's ribbon. Office Add-ins run in
Microsoft's trusted iframe — the host already enforces its own CSP.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add health check for bundled compiler executability

The musl-native GCC shipped in release bundles can't run on glibc
systems (Ubuntu) — it hangs because the kernel can't find the musl
dynamic linker. Previously oscan would try to use it and hang forever.

Fix: before accepting a bundled compiler, spawn it with --version and
wait up to 3 seconds. If it hangs or fails, skip it and fall back to
the host compiler. With --verbose, logs which candidates were skipped.

not installed. Use docker run with volume mount instead — works with
plain Docker on the agent, no extra plugin needed.

* fix: pin Docker Compose project name to prevent volume splitting

Docker Compose derives the project name from the directory name by default.
Running `docker compose` from different directories (e.g. `docker/` vs a
deployment dir) created separate named volumes, causing the OAuth config
stored in SQLite to be stranded in one volume while the app ran against a
different empty volume — prompting the setup wizard on every restart.

Changes:
- Add `name: the-matrix` to all three compose files so the project name
  is stable regardless of which directory `docker compose` runs from
- Add automatic volume migration to update.sh that detects orphaned
  volumes from previous project names and copies data + encryption keys
- Override still possible via COMPOSE_PROJECT_NAME or `-p` flag

* fix: use REST API fallback for guild membership check during startup

GetGuildInfoAsync relied on the DiscordSocketClient's in-memory cache,
but during OnStartupAsync the socket client hasn't connected yet (that
happens in ExecuteAsync). The empty cache caused the method to always
return null, producing a false 'Bot is not a member of server' error.

Fix: when the socket cache returns null, fall back to the Discord REST
API to verify guild membership. Also:
- Only return null for HTTP 404/403 (actual not-a-member); propagate
  other exceptions so transient failures aren't misreported
- Skip permission warnings when permissions are unknown (REST path);
  they'll be validated once the gateway connects
- Store bot token for REST fallback reuse
- Use CancellationToken via WaitAsync on the REST call

* fix: remove per-tick timeout that killed Discord gateway after 30 seconds

The PluginBackgroundTaskRunner applied a 30-second handler timeout to
ExecuteAsync via CancellationTokenSource.CancelAfter. This killed the
Discord gateway task 30 seconds after startup — only the startup
announcement got through before the task died.

The previous attempt to fix this (using PluginStopping token) didn't
work because the tickCts cancellation propagated through the
cancellationToken parameter regardless.

Root fix: ExecuteAsync now receives the plain shutdown token with no
per-tick timeout. Background tasks like the Discord gateway run for
their natural lifetime. The circuit breaker still handles crashes.

Also moves slash command registration from OnStartupAsync to
ExecuteAsync (after ConnectAsync), because RegisterSlashCommandsAsync
needs an active gateway connection to resolve the guild.

* fix: show real product photos when images exist, fall back to illustration only when none

* fix: reduce Router Cache staleTimes.static from 300s to 30s

The default staleTimes.static=300 matched the page revalidate=300,
causing Link-navigation to serve stale prefetched RSC payloads for up
to 5 minutes after server-side data changes (stock, images, prices).
Full reloads were fresh because they bypass the client Router Cache.

30s is the minimum Next.js allows and gives a good balance: prefetch
still works so navigation feels instant, but stale data windows shrink
from 5 min to 30 s — acceptable for a marketplace where product data
(images, stock, prices) changes frequently.

* ci: parallelize jobs and cache deps for faster pipeline

- All three jobs (verify/build/integration) now run in parallel; the
  needs: chain that gated integration behind verify+build is gone (no
  shared artifacts anyway).
- New composite action setup-deps caches node_modules + Prisma client
  keyed on package-lock + schema, so npm ci/prisma generate are skipped
  on cache hit.
- Verify runs typecheck:app, typecheck:test, and the test suite in
  parallel inside a single step, with grouped logs on completion.
- PRs run plain test:parallel; coverage only runs on main pushes.
- Build job now caches .next/cache between runs.

* ci: always run prisma generate (Prisma 7 outputs outside node_modules)

Prisma 7 generates the client into src/generated/prisma, which lives
outside the cached node_modules path. Skipping prisma generate on a
cache hit left the generated client missing, causing every Prisma type
to collapse to {} and ~500 typecheck errors.

Always run prisma generate (it's ~2s) and drop schema.prisma from the
cache key since it no longer affects what's cached.

fix: add GPU init timeout to prevent OOM-kill on Samsung during Gemma-4 load (#213)

* fix: add GPU init timeout to prevent OOM-kill on Samsung during Gemma-4 load

GPU initialization of Gemma-4 E4B takes ~50s on S23 Ultra and was being
OOM-killed by Samsung before the fallback chain could fire. Wrap each
backend's eng.initialize() in a CompletableFuture with a 25s timeout for
GPU (120s for CPU) so TimeoutException is caught and the loop falls back
to CPU within safe time bounds.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix: address code review issues in GPU init timeout

- Register whenComplete cleanup for engines that complete after timeout
  deadline (prevents GPU memory/weight leaks on Samsung)
- Wrap Error throwables (e.g. OutOfMemoryError) from supplyAsync in
  RuntimeException so the CPU fallback chain fires instead of crashing
- Handle InterruptedException explicitly in both tryInitEngine and
  createEngineWithFallback — restore interrupt flag and propagate
  immediately without attempting fallback backends

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* docs: add concurrent memory window note to tryInitEngine KDoc

Documents the known overlap window where GPU and CPU model weights can both
be resident after a GPU timeout. Explains why cancel(true) is absent and
suggests a future improvement with a serialised single-thread executor.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Add error boundary and auto-remediation for TV video player (#28)

The TV display can end up on an unrecoverable black screen when the
YouTube embed fails to load or stalls silently, requiring a manual page
refresh with no on-screen indication of what went wrong.

### Changes

- **React error boundary** (`tv-error-boundary.tsx`): Catches rendering
crashes in the TV player subtree. Shows a full-screen error UI with a
"Reload page" button that auto-clicks after a 5-second countdown. Any
user interaction (mouse/keyboard/touch) pauses the countdown to hand
control back to a human.

- **Video stall detection** (`tv-player.tsx`): Monitors `timeupdate`
events while `playing` is true. If no progress is reported for 15
seconds, assumes the embed is stuck and reloads the page.

- **Video error handler** (`tv-player.tsx`): Listens for `onError` from
the video element and triggers a reload after 3 seconds.

- **Wiring** (`tv-display.tsx`): Wraps `<TVPlayer>` in
`<TVErrorBoundary>`.

> **Note:** rwsdk's RSC bundler only recognizes function exports from
`"use client"` modules, so the class-based error boundary is wrapped in
an exported function component.
